CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Have a Defined Data Retention Policy
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Data Access**. Verifies that the agent has a documented data retention policy specifying the maximum number of days data is kept before automatic purge. Indefinite data retention increases privacy risk and regulatory exposure.
Risk: Without a retention policy, data collected by the agent may be stored indefinitely, increasing the blast radius of a breach and creating compliance violations under GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA data minimisation principles.

Code.Other:
1. Define a maximum retention period appropriate for the data classification (e.g., 90 days for logs, 30 days for conversation history).
2. Configure object lifecycle policies (S3, Azure Blob, GCS) to auto-delete data after the retention period.
3. Document the retention policy in the agent manifest and data inventory.

Recommendation.Text: Define and enforce a data retention policy for all data stores used by the agent. Automate purge via lifecycle rules and validate compliance regularly.

Notes: ASPM-051 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
